Three small backlog items closed in a single bug-bundle session. Second occurrence of the prototype-debris-on-v2-surfaces pattern (PR #48 first; PR #79 fold-in second) — new LESSON shipped for mobile-variant data-wiring + icon-only-no-behaviour affordance. Earlier same-day: PRs #70 (clearScanningData WRITE_TOO_BIG), #72 (Phase 6 PR 4 active-run surfacing), #73 (wifi-login security PR 1), #75 (post-PR-72 reflect + CLAUDE.md promotion of pre-flight write-path verification + pre-push self-review sub-steps), #76 (project-status.json sync).

sprint.goal: Effective implementation of Hi-Fi v2, with ROSS as the central post-login surface for every subscribed user
sprint.summary: Concierge-first IA. v1 admin tabs reframed as 'Ross's playbook' — the rules and procedures the future AI agent runs against. Phase 5 closed 2026-05-11. Phase 6 MAIN SCOPE COMPLETE 2026-05-19: all 5 PRs shipped — PR 1A tier-gating mechanism (#51), PR 1B curated 5/8 starter library (#53), PR 1C locked-card upsell UX (#55), PR 2 day-zero auto-activation (#58), PR 3 Run execution UI (#65), PR 4 concierge home active-run surfacing (#72). Workflows are the product; v1 modules become workflow step types over time. Two tiers only: Free (full UI + 5 starter templates), All-in (full library). Stretch task remains: Compliance Sweep starter template. Next sprint candidates: Phase 7 askRoss LLM, recurrence-engine gap (logged from PR #72 preview), Playbook/Activity stat-counter Missed/Overdue consistency.

phases.summary: Main scope complete 2026-05-19. PR 1A (#51) tier-gating mechanism. PR 1B (#53) curated 5 Free / 8 All-in split. PR 1C (#55) locked-card upsell UX + /upgrade.html. PR 2 (#58) day-zero auto-activation (mid-PR architecture pivot to dedicated rossSeedFirstWorkflow CF after preview revealed registerUser callable throwing unauthenticated). PR 4 (#72) concierge home active-run surfacing — new rossGetHomeWorkflowDigest CF + 5 card variants + LEARNING_MODE fallback + 'Missed' chip framing at daysLate >= 4 + home feed auto-reload on mount. Two preview-driven defects in PR #72 (nextDueDate number-not-string; runs have no status field) — same root-cause family as the validated 2026-05-01 SELF_OPT, second occurrence in 18 days. Stretch task (Compliance Sweep starter template) remains optional. Vocabulary per public/kb/features/ROSS.md: Templates → Workflows → Tasks → Runs.

WiFi login security hardening (PR 1 of 2) — new submitWifiLogin v2 onCall CF replaces direct-client RTDB writes; anonymous Firebase Auth + per-UID 5s debounce + shape validation + push-key sessionID + atomic multi-path write to wifiLogins/activeUsers/rateLimitsWifi; rules tightened from .write:auth!=null to admin-only on three PII nodes; open-redirect host-pattern whitelist on base_grant_url; client fallback chain preserves Meraki redirect on CF failure

clearScanningData chunked-delete + WRITE_TOO_BIG fix — admin Clear Scanning Data button hit RTDB ~16 MB single-write ceiling on grown /scanningData node; migrated CF from onRequest to v2 onCall + chunked delete via orderByKey().limitToFirst(500) + multi-path null update(); hard cap 200 batches/invocation; client loops across calls for larger nodes; folded tempClearData deletion (hardcoded secret, same bug)

registerUser callable Gen-2 signature fix (HIGH-sev bug-triage) — migrated v1 (data, context) => to v2 (request) =>; closed silent prod degradation that had been masking Path A code path since the firebase-functions v7 upgrade; pre-merge fold-in for HttpsError re-throw + KB doc sync; validated end-to-end on prod (positive + invalid-tier negative test)

bugs.title: Recurrence-engine gap: nextDueDate never advances when daily workflow goes uncompleted
bugs.notes: Operator noted on PR #72 preview: 'I see one workflow that hasn't been completed daily since it was created.' rossSubmitResponse auto-completes the run when all required tasks are done but does NOT advance the workflow's nextDueDate for the next cycle. A daily workflow created 10 days ago with no run completed reads as daysLate: 10 instead of rolling forward. Fix needs: (a) on run completion, advance nextDueDate by recurrence period; (b) decide policy when a cycle is fully missed (advance on next 'create run' attempt OR scheduled CF walks every workflow nightly). Touches rossSubmitResponse + possibly rossScheduledReminder. Backlog candidate for a dedicated sprint — recurrence semantics need product input.

bugs.title: Playbook + Activity stat counters — Missed/Overdue framing consistency with PR #72 home card
bugs.notes: PR #72 introduced threshold-based 'Missed' framing (daysLate >= 4) on the home concierge card. The Playbook tab's stat counter at RossPlaybook.vue:218-220 still reads 'Overdue' from store.overdueCount (counts workflows with locationStatus === 'overdue', no daysLate dimension). Activity tab likely has a similar counter. Operator caught the inconsistency post-merge. Two options for follow-up PR: (a) rename to 'Missed' globally; (b) split into two counters with daysLate-threshold computation mirroring the home card. Design call deferred.

bugs.title: Other 7 v1 callables in functions/index.js share the same Gen-2 signature bug (getQMSTierInfo, getQMSUsageStats, validateQMSFeatureAccess, validateQMSWhatsAppIntegration, performanceTest, runSystemOptimization, getSystemMetrics)
bugs.notes: All seven still use functions.https.onCall((data, context) => ...) and will throw unauthenticated on every call (same Gen-2 vs v1 signature mismatch fixed for registerUser in PR #67). Audit findings: two QMS callables show no recent invocations; the other five had logs-API errors on the audit pass. Consumer audit (public/): performanceTest has a working HTTP fallback (performanceTestHTTP); runSystemOptimization called only from admin-internal firebase-performance-monitor.js; QMS callables have no public/ consumers. None on user-facing critical path. Fix: same mechanical migration as PR #67 — onCall((request) => { const { data, auth } = request; ... }) with HttpsError from firebase-functions/v2/https. Recommend one sweep PR addressing all seven at once for consistent diff + single deploy.

bugs.title: Double-seed race on signup retry — rossSeedFirstWorkflow idempotency pre-check is not transactional
bugs.notes: A user double-clicking signup or hitting a network retry could pass the firstWorkflowSeededAt pre-check in both invocations and produce two seeded workflows. Mitigation options: (a) RTDB transaction() on the marker, OR (b) deterministic workflow ID per uid (e.g. seed_{uid}) so duplicate write is overwrite. Exposure small; consequence mild (duplicate workflow, no corruption).
